body:
When first fixing this bug, we changed the internal implementation of the URL sync to update the URL when a `search` event instead of a `change` event at the helper level. However this triggered a series of unforeseen side effects:
 - `search` only happens when there is an actual call to the `search()` method. Which prevents the update the URL to be updated when there is no actual search. When we want to hide most of the search interface when the query of the searchbox is empty, this makes it impossible to update the URL when it is empty without actually doing a search that would have no impact because we don't want to display the results then.
 - Using the `search` implies some tricks when using the back button (there is a [PR for implementing a state](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/pull/2174) for onPopState update)

Overall it feels like using this approach was not the good one as we have to continue fixing the multiple issues. However, we could also try to make sure that we don't trigger a JS Helper state when the `searchOnEnterKeyPressOnly` option is activated. This way we could go back to using the simpler implementation of the URL sync based on the `change` event.
